name: Parker House Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1/2 stick butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast and sugar in the warm water; let proof.
2. Melt the butter in the warm milk, then combine with the yeast mixture in a large mixing bowl.
3. Mix 2-3 cups of flour with salt, adding it to the mixture in the bowl, stirring vigorously with a wooden spoon to form a soft sponge. The dough will be wet and sticky.
4. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap; place in a warm location and let rise until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour).
5. Stir down with a wooden spoon and add another 2 cups of flour, mixing 1 cup at a time, until the dough can be kneaded easily.
6. Turn out onto a lightly floured board and knead until velvety smooth and very elastic; press with fingers to ensure resilience.
7. Rest for a few minutes, then form into a ball. Place in a buttered bowl, turning so that the surface is covered with butter.
8. Cover and place in a warm, draft-free area to rise again until doubled in bulk.
9. Punch down with your fist; turn out onto a lightly floured board.
10. Let rest several minutes until it can be rolled out to 1/2 inch thickness.
11. Cut rounds using a 2- or 2-1/2-inch cutter, or by pressing water glasses coated in flour.
12. Brush the center of each round with melted butter; create a deep indentation without breaking through the dough.
13. Fold over one-third of each round and press to seal.
14. Arrange on a buttered baking sheet about 1/2 inch apart.
15. Brush again with melted butter and bake in a preheated oven at 375°F until lightly browned (about 20 minutes). Test by gently tapping; if you hear a faint hollow sound, or the roll is cooked inside when broken open, it's done.
16. Serve hot from the oven with plenty of butter and preserves or honey, if desired.
